mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month rise in FEDFUNDS signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect strengthens monotonically with the cumulative size of the rate increase.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS has been on a hiking trajectory into April 2005
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and forward 20d SPX return stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window, or returns during rising-rate periods are not significantly lower than during flat/falling periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window from a cyclical low, it signals an inflection in the labor market that historically precedes broader earnings deceleration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter (60 trading days). Below this threshold, the low-and-stable unemployment regime supports continued equity gains.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% in April 2005, near cycle lows
failure_condition: In periods when the trigger fires, forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the trigger fires fewer than 5 times in the historical sample making the signal untestable.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in HOUST (housing starts) and RSAFS (retail sales) jointly indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion that individually each series only partially captures; when both accelerate together, forward-looking earnings revisions turn positive and S&P 500 returns rise over the next 20 trading days more than either series predicts alone.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1364k units and RSAFS at 526057 million in early 2005 snapshot
failure_condition: The joint-trigger signal's forward return distribution is statistically indistinguishable from the union of single-series signals (interaction term coefficient not significant at p<0.10) in walk-forward regression.

mechanism_narrative: During mid-cycle expansions, a positive 6-month trend in INDPRO reflects healthy industrial capacity utilization growth that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter (60 trading days). This relationship is regime-dependent: in late-cycle or recessionary regimes, rising INDPRO can instead signal overheating and precede corrections.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO trend is not associated with positive forward 60d returns in a statistically significant way (Rank IC < 0.03) when restricted to independently-identified mid-cycle expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPIAUCSL inflation crosses above 3%, markets price in higher probability of accelerated Fed tightening, causing an immediate risk-off repricing of equities and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days. Below this threshold, inflation surprises are absorbed without material equity market disruption.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL index at 256.358 in March 2005 reflecting elevated inflation readings
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns following trigger events are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or the sign flips positive in more than 50% of trigger instances across the walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects ongoing labor market expansion that supports consumer income and spending, translating into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The relationship holds continuously without requiring a threshold, as payroll growth is a persistent leading indicator of aggregate demand.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 151722 thousand persons in March 2005, consistent with expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth rate and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS rising), a simultaneous decline in HOUST amplifies the negative equity impact beyond what either signal predicts alone, because contracting housing activity combined with rising rates signals compounding stress on rate-sensitive sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter (60 trading days). Outside tightening regimes, HOUST declines alone have muted equity impact.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1364k units while FEDFUNDS on hiking path into April 2005
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS trend and HOUST decline is not statistically significant (p>0.10) relative to the additive combination of single-series effects in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month annualized growth rate of INDPRO crosses from positive to negative, it signals an abrupt deceleration in industrial output that triggers rapid downward revision of near-term earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth crosses from positive to negative
failure_condition: Forward 5d SPX returns following the crossing event are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the crossing event occurs too infrequently (<8 times) to support statistical inference in the historical sample.
